title: Know what to expect from an education direction
description: If you breach the law, we may ask you to complete an SMSF education course.
keywords: SMSF education direction, education direction notice, SMSF trustee breach, SMSF non-compliance actions, superannuation law breach, trustee obligations SMSF, director of corporate trustee SMSF, approved SMSF education course, complete SMSF education course, trustee declaration NAT 71089, sign trustee declaration, SMSF compliance requirements, consequences of breaching super laws, ATO SMSF education direction, SMSF regulatory action, get back on track SMSF, SMSF trustee responsibilities, Our SMSF non-compliance actions, SMSF newsroom, SMSF newsletter

content_blocks.text: If you breach your obligations under the law when operating your SMSF, we may ask you to complete an SMSF education course.
We’ve updated our non-compliance actions webpage to include clearer guidance about education directions – one of the tools we use to help trustees get back on track when they breach the law.

An education direction is a formal notice requiring a trustee (or director of a corporate trustee) to complete an approved SMSF education course within a set time. This helps ensure they understand their legal obligations and responsibilities going forward.
When someone receives an education direction, they must:

items: complete the course by the due date, and
sign a new trustee declaration (NAT 71089) within 21 days.

content_blocks.text: This update aims to make it easier for trustees to understand what the direction involves – and what they need to do next.
For more information on the actions we take if you don't comply with super laws visit Our SMSF non-compliance actions .
Looking for the latest news for SMSFs? You can stay up to date by visiting our SMSF newsroom and subscribing External Link to our monthly SMSF newsletter.

content_text:
If you breach your obligations under the law when operating your SMSF, we may ask you to complete an SMSF education course.
We’ve updated our non-compliance actions webpage to include clearer guidance about education directions – one of the tools we use to help trustees get back on track when they breach the law.
An education direction is a formal notice requiring a trustee (or director of a corporate trustee) to complete an approved SMSF education course within a set time. This helps ensure they understand their legal obligations and responsibilities going forward.
When someone receives an education direction, they must:
- complete the course by the due date, and
- sign a new trustee declaration (NAT 71089) within 21 days.
This update aims to make it easier for trustees to understand what the direction involves – and what they need to do next.
For more information on the actions we take if you don't comply with super laws visit Our SMSF non-compliance actions .
Looking for the latest news for SMSFs? You can stay up to date by visiting our SMSF newsroom and subscribing External Link to our monthly SMSF newsletter.
